name: Aria Automation: Project Has No Cloud Zone
description: An Aria Automation project cannot deploy blueprints because no cloud zone has been assigned to it, causing all deployment requests to fail at scheduling.
customerScenario: Our developers are trying to deploy from the Aria Automation service catalog and every request is failing immediately. The error says something like 'Unable to provision — no resources available in this project'. The exact same blueprints work fine in a different project. We just created this project last week for a new team and it's blocking their work.
customerFollowUp: The infrastructure team set up the project. They created it in Aria Automation and added the right team members and roles. But I think they followed an old wiki page from before we added some extra configuration steps. Something in the project setup might have been skipped.
fixSteps: Log in to Aria Automation as an administrator
Open the failing project and select the Provisioning tab
Confirm that no cloud zones are listed under 'Cloud zones and accounts'
Click Add Zone and select the appropriate cloud zone for your environment
Set the priority (1 = highest precedence) and optionally configure resource limits
Re-submit a deployment request from the service catalog — it should now proceed past scheduling
hints: The error 'no resources available in this project' means Aria Automation's scheduling engine has nowhere to place the workload. Aria Automation uses projects to scope resources — only cloud zones assigned to a project are available for deployments within it. Where in the Aria Automation admin UI do you control which resources a project can use?

In Aria Automation, navigate to Infrastructure > Projects and open the failing project. Projects have a Provisioning tab that controls which cloud zones are available. Check whether any cloud zones appear under 'Cloud zones and accounts'.

The project has no cloud zones assigned. Without a cloud zone, Aria Automation cannot determine where to deploy VMs or other infrastructure — the deployment request fails at scheduling before any blueprint logic runs. The fix is to add the correct cloud zone.

In the project's Provisioning tab, click Add Zone. You'll see the cloud zones configured in your environment — select the vSphere (or VCF) cloud zone. Set the priority to 1 if it's the only zone. Resource limits are optional but useful for capacity governance.

Add the cloud zone: Infrastructure > Projects > [project name] > Provisioning tab > Add Zone > select your vSphere cloud zone > set priority 1 > Save. Re-submit a deployment from the service catalog — it should now proceed to provisioning instead of failing at scheduling.
learningObjectives: Understand the relationship between Aria Automation projects and cloud zones
Diagnose 'no resources available' deployment failures by inspecting project provisioning configuration
Assign a cloud zone to a project via Infrastructure > Projects
Verify the fix by confirming a deployment request proceeds past the scheduling stage
author: vSphere Lab Wizard starter library
